In Umbanda ceremonies, mediums incorporate these spirits, who then give consultations, counsel, blessings, and spiritual cures to those who come, working off negative energies and offering guidance. Sessions involve drumming, singing, ritual cleansing, and spiritual 'passes'. Umbanda spread across southern Brazil and into neighbouring countries and is one of Brazil's major religions.

sources.title: The Meeting of Paths: Allan Kardec, Umbanda & Candomble, Santo Daime, and Ayahuasca in Brazil
sources.mla: "The Meeting of Paths: Allan Kardec, Umbanda & Candomble, Santo Daime, and Ayahuasca in Brazil." MaraVilha, https://www.maravilharetreat.com/blog/maravilha-retreat/maravilharetreat/blog-page-url/ayahuasca. Accessed 14 July 2026.

gods.name: Olorun
gods.description: The one supreme God, creator of all, identified with the Christian God and standing above the orixas and all the spirits. As in the African-rooted traditions, remote and not directly petitioned; devotion flows through the orixas and the working spirits.

gods.name: The orixas
gods.domain: The forces of nature, divine governance
gods.description: The deities inherited from the African and Candomble tradition, governing the forces of nature and often identified with Catholic saints. In Umbanda they are venerated but somewhat less central to daily practice than the working spirits of the dead, who do the direct labour of healing and counsel.

gods.name: The pretos velhos
gods.domain: Wisdom, patience, healing, the enslaved ancestors
gods.description: The spirits of old enslaved Africans, among the most beloved figures in Umbanda: patient, humble, deeply wise, and compassionate, speaking in the manner of elderly former slaves and offering folk wisdom, consolation, and healing through the mediums who incorporate them. They embody endurance and grace under suffering.

gods.name: The caboclos
gods.domain: The forest, strength, herbal healing, indigenous knowledge
gods.description: The proud and vigorous spirits of indigenous Brazilians, masters of the forest and of medicinal plants, who work through mediums with strength and directness, offering protection, energetic cleansing, and cures. Together with the pretos velhos they represent the two great lines of Umbanda's working spirits.

gods.name: The crianças
gods.description: The child-spirits, playful and innocent, who bring lightness, joy, and a particular gentle healing, especially associated with children and with the dissolving of heavy energies through their cheerfulness. Honoured especially around the Catholic feast of Cosmas and Damian.

umbanda.godsNote: Umbanda is a twentieth-century Brazilian synthesis of Candomble's orixas, Catholicism, Kardecist Spiritism, and indigenous traditions, and it varies enormously across its many branches, some closer to Candomble and Africa, some closer to Kardecist Spiritism and Christianity. It is monotheistic in acknowledging one supreme God, with the orixas and the working spirits of the dead beneath. 'Macumba' is a related term often used pejoratively for Umbanda and Candomble.

santoDaime.name:
Santo
Daime
santoDaime.description: Santo Daime is a syncretic Brazilian religion founded in the 1930s in the Amazonian state of Acre by Raimundo Irineu Serra, a Black rubber-tapper of great height and presence known as Mestre Irineu, who had learned the ritual use of ayahuasca, the visionary Amazonian brew, from indigenous and mestizo vegetalismo traditions. In his central visionary experience, Mestre Irineu encountered a female divine figure, the Queen of the Forest, identified with the Virgin Mary, who instructed him to found the religion and, when he protested that he was illiterate and could not sing, told him to open his mouth, giving him the first hymn. From that beginning, Santo Daime became a 'musical doctrine': its scriptures are not a written text but the hinarios, collections of hymns 'received' or channeled by Mestre Irineu and, after him, by the leaders (padrinhos and madrinhas) and members, whose melodies and words together carry the teaching. The sacrament is Daime, ayahuasca itself, drunk in long ceremonies called 'works' (trabalhos) that may last many hours, in which participants, dressed in white or, for some rites, blue uniforms, sit in silent concentration or dance in simple synchronized steps in geometric formation while singing the hymns collectively. The religion fuses folk Catholicism (the Cross, the Virgin, the saints, Christ), Kardecist Spiritism (reincarnation, spirit communication), African-Brazilian elements (some churches, in the 'Umbandaime' current, incorporate Umbanda spirits), and Amazonian shamanism, all organized around Irineu's motto of harmony, love, truth, and justice. It spread from the Amazon across Brazil and, from the 1990s, worldwide, winning legal recognition for its sacramental ayahuasca use in several countries.

sources.title: Mestre Irineu: A Black Man Who Changed the History of Ayahuasca
sources.mla: "Mestre Irineu: A Black Man Who Changed the History of Ayahuasca." Chacruna, https://chacruna.net/mestre-irineu-ayahuasca/. Accessed 14 July 2026.

gods.name: God
gods.domain: Creation, the source, the divine order
gods.description: Santo Daime is built on a folk-Catholic foundation, and its supreme God is the Christian one, worshipped through the Cross, the hymns, and the ceremonies, within a framework that also embraces reincarnation and spirit communication from Kardecist Spiritism.

gods.name: The Queen of the Forest
gods.domain: Revelation, the forest, the founding of the doctrine
gods.description: The female divine figure, identified with the Virgin Mary, who appeared to Mestre Irineu in his ayahuasca visions and commanded him to found Santo Daime, giving him the first hymn when he protested he could not sing. She presides over the doctrine as its founding revelation and its guiding presence.
gods.imageQuery: Queen of the Forest Santo Daime Virgin

gods.name: Christ and the saints
gods.description: Christ and the Catholic saints are venerated within the doctrine, their feast days and imagery woven into the ritual calendar and the hymns. Santo Daime presents itself as a Christian path, with the Daime sacrament as the means of direct spiritual experience of these figures and teachings.
gods.imageQuery: Santo Daime hymn ceremony white uniforms

gods.name: The Daime
gods.description: Not a deity but the sacred centre of the religion: ayahuasca, understood as a divine gift and even, in the vegetalista background, as a teacher-being in its own right, through which participants receive healing, moral discipline, and direct visionary contact with the spiritual world. The 'works' are structured entirely around drinking it and singing.

santoDaime.godsNote: Santo Daime is a Christian-centred syncretic religion whose sacrament is ayahuasca and whose 'scripture' is a body of channeled hymns. It fuses folk Catholicism, Kardecist Spiritism, Afro-Brazilian religion (in the Umbandaime current), and Amazonian shamanism. Its ritual use of ayahuasca has been the subject of legal cases in several countries, generally resolved in the religion's favour as protected religious practice.
imageQueries: Santo Daime ceremony white uniforms Brazil
